subject: math
prompt: The population of a certain city doubled every 25 years from 1660 to 1760. The population of this city was 208,000 in 1760. What was the population of this city in 1660?
questionText: The population of a certain city doubled every 25 years from 1660 to 1760. The population of this city was 208,000 in 1760. What was the population of this city in 1660?

subject: math
prompt: The function $f(x)=600(0.5)^{\frac{x}{11}}$ gives the predicted intensity of a beam $f(x)$, in number of photons in the beam, $x$ millimeters below the surface of a certain material. What is the best interpretation of $f(11)= 300$?
questionText: The function $f(x)=600(0.5)^{\frac{x}{11}}$ gives the predicted intensity of a beam $f(x)$, in number of photons in the beam, $x$ millimeters below the surface of a certain material. What is the best interpretation of $f(11)= 300$?

choices.text: A beam at the surface of the material has a predicted intensity of 11 photons in the beam.
A beam at the surface of the material has a predicted intensity of 300 photons in the beam.
A beam 11 millimeters below the surface of the material has a predicted intensity of 300 photons in the beam.
A beam 300 millimeters below the surface of the material has a predicted intensity of 600 photons in the beam.

subject: math
prompt: A computer program models the population of a certain insect in an environment where the insect has no natural predators. According to the model, the estimated total mass of the population of insects at the end of every 5-week period is 158% greater than the estimated total mass of the population of insects at the end of the previous 5-week period. The estimated total mass of the population of insects at the end of 15 weeks is 627.7 grams. Which equation best represents this model, where $M$ is the estimated total mass, in grams, of the population of insects at the end of $t$ weeks?
questionText: A computer program models the population of a certain insect in an environment where the insect has no natural predators. According to the model, the estimated total mass of the population of insects at the end of every 5-week period is 158% greater than the estimated total mass of the population of insects at the end of the previous 5-week period. The estimated total mass of the population of insects at the end of 15 weeks is 627.7 grams. Which equation best represents this model, where $M$ is the estimated total mass, in grams, of the population of insects at the end of $t$ weeks?

According to this model, at the end of week $w$ after the initial observation, where $w \gt 2$, which of the following functions gives the predicted number of bees still needed to reach the beekeeper's goal?

prompt: A group of 80 employees selected at random from all the employees at a company were surveyed about the number of books they read last year. Which of the following is the most appropriate conclusion?
questionText: A group of 80 employees selected at random from all the employees at a company were surveyed about the number of books they read last year. From the data collected, it was estimated that the mean number of books employees at the company read last year is 7, with an associated margin of error of 1.6. Which of the following is the most appropriate conclusion?

choices.text: It is plausible that the actual mean number of books employees at the company read last year is less than 5.4.
It is plausible that the actual mean number of books employees at the company read last year is between 5.4 and 8.6.
It is plausible that every employee at the company read between 7 and 11.8 books last year.
It is plausible that the actual mean number of books employees at the company read last year is greater than 8.6.

subject: math
prompt: The roofs on the buildings of a public garden are designed to allow water to drain into the garden. The table shows the relationship between the area $x$, in square feet, of a roof and the amount of water $f(x)$, in gallons, drained from the roof into the garden over a certain period of time. Which equation could define $f$?
questionText: The roofs on the buildings of a public garden are designed to allow water to drain into the garden. The table shows the relationship between the area $x$, in square feet, of a roof and the amount of water $f(x)$, in gallons, drained from the roof into the garden over a certain period of time. Which equation could define $f$?

subject: math
prompt: Each day Daniela takes a 30-minute morning walk. The equation $s = 70m + 285$ models the total number of steps, $s$, Daniela has taken one morning $m$ minutes after the start of her walk, where $0 \lt m \lt 30$. Which is the best interpretation of 285 in this context?
questionText: Each day Daniela takes a 30-minute morning walk. The equation $s = 70m + 285$ models the total number of steps, $s$, Daniela has taken one morning $m$ minutes after the start of her walk, where $0 \lt m \lt 30$. Which is the best interpretation of 285 in this context?

choices.text: The estimated total number of steps Daniela will take during her walk
The estimated total number of steps Daniela will have taken after her walk
The estimated number of steps Daniela takes each minute during her walk
The estimated number of steps Daniela had taken that morning before starting her walk

category.subject: Math
category.skill: Linear inequalities in one or two variables
